title: Bellevalia theraea
intro: Bellevalia theraea, also known as Theran honey garlic, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asparagaceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Turkey and the Middle East, to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems, hypertension, and digestive disorders. The bioactive constituents of Bellevalia theraea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Bellevalia theraea include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are used to promote cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
taxonomy: Bellevalia theraea is a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae. It belongs to the genus Bellevalia and is a part of the Asparagales order. Within the Asparagaceae family, it is classified as part of the subfamily Scilloideae. The subfamily Scilloideae is further divided into several tribes, with Bellevalia theraea being a part of the Scilleae tribe. The classification of Bellevalia theraea is an essential aspect of botanical taxonomy.
